OPPOSITION OF PLTF'S TO DEFTS' MOTION FOR PROECTIVE ORDER; DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO DEFTS' MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER

DISCOVERY 612, DEFENDANT AMIT MOTAWALA, ILA MOTAWALA NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED. AS STATED IN THE CASES DEFENDANTS' THEMSELVES CITE, A COURT HAS NO POWER TO GRANT A PROTECTIVE ORDER BARRING A PARTY FROM ATTENDING ANOTHER'S DEPOSITION. MOREOVER, THE PROTECTIVE ORDER SOUGHT BY DEFENDANT WOULD BE INEFFECTIVE EVEN IF GRANTED, AS THE PARTIES COULD NONETHELESS REVIEW TRANSCRIPTS OF OTHER PARTIES' DEPOSITIONS. JUDGE RICHARD B. ULMER, JR. NOT REPORTED

Off Calendar Jun-17-2010 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion For Leave To File A First-Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-14-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:

PLAINTIFF LINDA JONES, VICTORIA JACKSON, CAROLYN HUGHES, ANESHA MARSHALL, THOMAS DAVID, ANINA MATTHEWS' Notice Of Motion For Leave To File A First-Amended Complaint. PLAINTIFF MAY AMEND THEIR COMPLAINT WITH THE EXCEPTION OF THE REFERENCE TO SECTION 37.10B (A)(13), BECAUSE THE PROPOSED AMENDMENT MERELY ADDS AN ADDITIONAL BASIS FOR RELIEF AS TO THE SAME FACTS ALREADY AT ISSUE IN THE CASE. BY PERMITTING THESE ALLEGATIIONS, THE COURT DOES NOT INDICATE ANY INTENTION TO ENTERTAIN A RENEWED CLASS CERTIFICATION MOTION, THE PRIOR ONE HAVING BEEN DENIED ON ITS MERITS, AND THESE AMENDMENTS NOT IN ANY WAY AFFECTING THE MERITS OF CLASS CERTIFICATION. NOR DOES THE COURT INTEND TO SUGGEST THAT ANY TRIAL CONTINUANCE IS NECESSARY OR APPROPRIATE SINCE THE ONLY POTENTIAL NEW ISSUE, RELATING TO A CLAIMED INVASION OF PRIVACY IS NOT BEING ALLOWED GIVEN THE IMMINENCE OF TRIAL, THE PREJUDICE THAT WOULD RESULT TO DEFENDANTS, LACK OF ANY REASONABLE EXCUSE FOR NOT HAVING ASSERTED SUCH A CLAIM SOONER, THE LACK OF ANY FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS IN SUPPORT OF SUCH A CLAIM, AND PLAINTIFF'S EXPRESSED WILLINGNESS TO OMIT THAT ALLEGATION. Moving party to submit form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 = (301/PJB)

Notice Ofm Otion An Dmotion For Protective Order;
Off Calendar Feb-18-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Michelle D. Jew, Atty. for defendant and moving party: Amit Motawala
No appearances made by Plantiff.

RULING: DEFENDANT AMIT MOTAWALA, ILA MOTAWALA Notice Of Motion And motion For Protective Order is DENIED. As stated in the cases Defendants' themselves cite, a court has no power to grant a protective order barring a party from attending another's deposition. Moreover, the protective order sought by Defendant would be ineffective even if granted, as the parties could nonetheless review transcripts of other parties' depositions. JUDGE RICHARD B. ULMER, JR. NOT REPORTED
